text.name: CTO REVIEW / AURORA ENERGY RESEARCH
text.text: CTO REVIEW / AURORA ENERGY RESEARCH

text.name: Reopen as capability risk, not as a Mac standard
text.text: Reopen as capability risk, not as a Mac standard

text.name: CURRENT DECISION
text.text: CURRENT DECISION

text.name: Pilot only, no rollout
text.text: Pilot only, no rollout

text.name: Default remains Lenovo Windows plus WSL2. The visual evidence does justify a bounded investigation because the workflow risk now touches Aurora AI-native product development.
text.text: Default remains Lenovo Windows plus WSL2. The visual evidence does justify a bounded investigation because the workflow risk now touches Aurora AI-native product development.

text.name: WHAT THE VISUALS PROVE
text.text: WHAT THE VISUALS PROVE

text.name: WHERE I PUSH BACK
text.text: WHERE I PUSH BACK

text.name: HOW I WOULD DECIDE
text.text: HOW I WOULD DECIDE

text.name: There is real workflow friction
text.text: There is real workflow friction

text.name: The endpoint answer is still unproven
text.text: The endpoint answer is still unproven

text.name: Run a bounded capability test
text.text: Run a bounded capability test

text.name: - The boundary diagram shows the loop now crossing Windows GUI and WSL continuously, not occasionally.
text.text: - The boundary diagram shows the loop now crossing Windows GUI and WSL continuously, not occasionally.

text.name: - The visuals prove friction, but not that macOS is the only acceptable control plane.
text.text: - The visuals prove friction, but not that macOS is the only acceptable control plane.

text.name: Define critical loops: canvas read/write, prototype critique, repo-agent work, research synthesis, markdown context and verification.
text.text: Define critical loops: canvas read/write, prototype critique, repo-agent work, research synthesis, markdown context and verification.

text.name: Time-box IT and InfoSec discovery to 30 to 45 days, with explicit controls and support model.
text.text: Time-box IT and InfoSec discovery to 30 to 45 days, with explicit controls and support model.

text.name: - The Figma case maps live canvas use cases to multiple failed or fragile implementation paths.
text.text: - The Figma case maps live canvas use cases to multiple failed or fragile implementation paths.

text.name: - The deck needs measured drag: hours lost, failed loops, support incidents, delayed decisions and onboarding cost.
text.text: - The deck needs measured drag: hours lost, failed loops, support incidents, delayed decisions and onboarding cost.

text.name: Run one-seat pilot with baseline metrics: setup time, failed loop rate, manual bridge fixes, cycle time and support hours.
text.text: Run one-seat pilot with baseline metrics: setup time, failed loop rate, manual bridge fixes, cycle time and support hours.

text.name: - The WSLg architecture slide removes the simple Linux GUI fallback I would otherwise test first.
text.text: - The WSLg architecture slide removes the simple Linux GUI fallback I would otherwise test first.

text.name: - InfoSec work for Macs is real platform work: identity, device management, patching, backup, recovery and compliance.
text.text: - InfoSec work for Macs is real platform work: identity, device management, patching, backup, recovery and compliance.

text.name: Decide at architecture review: UX-specific macOS endpoint, productised Windows/WSL bridge, or hosted/browser-first agent path.
text.text: Decide at architecture review: UX-specific macOS endpoint, productised Windows/WSL bridge, or hosted/browser-first agent path.

text.name: - The scaling topology shows the issue being inherited by UXE, UX and UR hires, not contained to one expert.
text.text: - The scaling topology shows the issue being inherited by UXE, UX and UR hires, not contained to one expert.

text.name: - A 4-person UX exception can quietly become an unmanaged second endpoint standard without governance.
text.text: - A 4-person UX exception can quietly become an unmanaged second endpoint standard without governance.

text.name: Guardrail: do not let device preference bypass architecture. Decide the operating model first, then the hardware.
text.text: Guardrail: do not let device preference bypass architecture. Decide the operating model first, then the hardware.

text.name: Implication
text.text: Implication

text.name: Decision gap
text.text: Decision gap

text.name: This is enough to reopen the decision. It is no longer credible to treat the request only as personal hardware preference.
text.text: This is enough to reopen the decision. It is no longer credible to treat the request only as personal hardware preference.

text.name: I need Mac versus Windows/WSL versus hosted agent workspace compared on total friction, total cost and residual security risk.
text.text: I need Mac versus Windows/WSL versus hosted agent workspace compared on total friction, total cost and residual security risk.

text.name: ULTIMATE CONCLUSION
text.text: ULTIMATE CONCLUSION

text.name: I would not approve a Mac rollout for the expanding UX team on this deck alone. I would reopen the request because the visual workflow evidence links endpoint choice to Aurora AI-native product objectives. Authorize bounded IT and InfoSec discovery plus one pilot. If macOS proves materially lower-risk and higher-throughput under enterprise controls, create a UX-specific standard; otherwise invest in a supported Windows/WSL or hosted agent path.
text.text: I would not approve a Mac rollout for the expanding UX team on this deck alone. I would reopen the request because the visual workflow evidence links endpoint choice to Aurora AI-native product objectives. Authorize bounded IT and InfoSec discovery plus one pilot. If macOS proves materially lower-risk and higher-throughput under enterprise controls, create a UX-specific standard; otherwise invest in a supported Windows/WSL or hosted agent path.

text.name: Case reopened. Approval conditional.
text.text: Case reopened. Approval conditional.

text.name: SUPPORTING CHALLENGE LOG / CTO REVIEW
text.text: SUPPORTING CHALLENGE LOG / CTO REVIEW

text.name: Specific challenges to the Mac approval case
text.text: Specific challenges to the Mac approval case

text.name: REVIEW STANCE
text.text: REVIEW STANCE

text.name: Friction proven, remedy unproven
text.text: Friction proven, remedy unproven

text.name: The deck makes a credible case for workflow friction. These are the questions I would force before turning that into a supported macOS endpoint standard.
text.text: The deck makes a credible case for workflow friction. These are the questions I would force before turning that into a supported macOS endpoint standard.

text.name: Macs do not create a control plane
text.text: Macs do not create a control plane

text.name: Figma pain may be overgeneralized
text.text: Figma pain may be overgeneralized

text.name: macOS may remove the WSL boundary, but live canvas automation still depends on app APIs, MCP compatibility, agent permissions, local sockets and tool-specific bridge work.
text.text: macOS may remove the WSL boundary, but live canvas automation still depends on app APIs, MCP compatibility, agent permissions, local sockets and tool-specific bridge work.

text.name: The bridge evidence is strong, but it could be a Figma integration problem, a local-agent architecture problem, or an endpoint policy problem. The deck collapses those too quickly.
text.text: The bridge evidence is strong, but it could be a Figma integration problem, a local-agent architecture problem, or an endpoint policy problem. The deck collapses those too quickly.

text.name: No quantified business loss yet
text.text: No quantified business loss yet

text.name: Security cost is underplayed
text.text: Security cost is underplayed

text.name: I would ask for weekly hours lost, failed loop count, support incidents, manual workaround time, delayed decisions, onboarding drag and any delivery impact.
text.text: I would ask for weekly hours lost, failed loop count, support incidents, manual workaround time, delayed decisions, onboarding drag and any delivery impact.

text.name: Mac support means MDM, EDR, DLP, identity, certificates, patching, backup, recovery, procurement, app governance, audit evidence and support readiness.
text.text: Mac support means MDM, EDR, DLP, identity, certificates, patching, backup, recovery, procurement, app governance, audit evidence and support readiness.

text.name: Local endpoint integration is assumed
text.text: Local endpoint integration is assumed

text.name: Scaling cuts both ways
text.text: Scaling cuts both ways

text.name: The deck assumes the future is local app plus local agent. I would compare hosted agent workspaces, cloud dev environments and internal gateway services.
text.text: The deck assumes the future is local app plus local agent. I would compare hosted agent workspaces, cloud dev environments and internal gateway services.

text.name: A 4-person UX team makes the workflow issue more material, but also creates a second platform surface unless procurement, security and support are explicit.
text.text: A 4-person UX team makes the workflow issue more material, but also creates a second platform surface unless procurement, security and support are explicit.

text.name: Hero-workflow bias
text.text: Hero-workflow bias

text.name: Windows remediation is not exhausted
text.text: Windows remediation is not exhausted

text.name: The current UX lead is unusually technical. The decision should test whether ordinary UX and UR hires can use the model without debugging bridges.
text.text: The current UX lead is unusually technical. The decision should test whether ordinary UX and UR hires can use the model without debugging bridges.

text.name: Before approving Macs, compare a productised Windows-native agent service, approved localhost policy, managed tunnel, Figma plugin path or MCP gateway.
text.text: Before approving Macs, compare a productised Windows-native agent service, approved localhost policy, managed tunnel, Figma plugin path or MCP gateway.

text.name: AI-native is too broad as a policy test
text.text: AI-native is too broad as a policy test

text.name: Precedent risk is real
text.text: Precedent risk is real

text.name: Almost any advanced tooling request can use that frame. Approval criteria need to define when endpoint exceptions are justified and when platform investment is better.
text.text: Almost any advanced tooling request can use that frame. Approval criteria need to define when endpoint exceptions are justified and when platform investment is better.

text.name: If UX gets Macs for AI workflow reasons, engineering, data science, research and power users may make adjacent claims. The boundary must be explicit.
text.text: If UX gets Macs for AI workflow reasons, engineering, data science, research and power users may make adjacent claims. The boundary must be explicit.

text.name: The deck proves the current setup creates meaningful friction and should be reopened. It does not yet prove that macOS is the lowest-risk, highest-leverage remedy. The next decision should compare macOS, productised Windows/WSL support and hosted agent infrastructure against measured workflow outcomes and enterprise control cost.
text.text: The deck proves the current setup creates meaningful friction and should be reopened. It does not yet prove that macOS is the lowest-risk, highest-leverage remedy. The next decision should compare macOS, productised Windows/WSL support and hosted agent infrastructure against measured workflow outcomes and enterprise control cost.

text.name: SHARPEST CHALLENGE
text.text: SHARPEST CHALLENGE

text.name: Friction proven. Remedy not yet proven.
text.text: Friction proven. Remedy not yet proven.

case.question: How should Aurora decide whether to support macOS hardware for the expanding UX team?

answer1.label: Design Org
answer1.position: Invest in the workflow path that unlocks design productivity.

answer2.label: Platform Org
answer2.position: Standardise around the lowest operational burden until evidence clears the bar.

sides.label: Design Org
sides.position: Invest in the workflow path that unlocks design productivity.

sides.label: Platform Org
sides.position: Standardise around the lowest operational burden until evidence clears the bar.

evidence.summary:
Figma file: UX Strategy
Page: WSL/Windows
Slides reviewed: 2
Evidence includes extracted text and frame metadata.
Screenshots are attempted by default for visual context; review continues if capture is unavailable.

slides.name: CTO Review - Mac Hardware Request
text: CTO REVIEW / AURORA ENERGY RESEARCH
Reopen as capability risk, not as a Mac standard
CURRENT DECISION
Pilot only, no rollout
Default remains Lenovo Windows plus WSL2. The visual evidence does justify a bounded investigation because the workflow risk now touches Aurora AI-native product development.
WHAT THE VISUALS PROVE
WHERE I PUSH BACK
HOW I WOULD DECIDE
There is real workflow friction
The endpoint answer is still unproven
Run a bounded capability test
- The boundary diagram shows the loop now crossing Windows GUI and WSL continuously, not occasionally.
- The visuals prove friction, but not that macOS is the only acceptable control plane.
Define critical loops: canvas read/write, prototype critique, repo-agent work, research synthesis, markdown context and verification.
Time-box IT and InfoSec discovery to 30 to 45 days, with explicit controls and support model.
- The Figma case maps live canvas use cases to multiple failed or fragile implementation paths.
- The deck needs measured drag: hours lost, failed loops, support incidents, delayed decisions and onboarding cost.
Run one-seat pilot with baseline metrics: setup time, failed loop rate, manual bridge fixes, cycle time and support hours.
- The WSLg architecture slide removes the simple Linux GUI fallback I would otherwise test first.
- InfoSec work for Macs is real platform work: identity, device management, patching, backup, recovery and compliance.
Decide at architecture review: UX-specific macOS endpoint, productised Windows/WSL bridge, or hosted/browser-first agent path.
- The scaling topology shows the issue being inherited by UXE, UX and UR hires, not contained to one expert.
- A 4-person UX exception can quietly become an unmanaged second endpoint standard without governance.
Guardrail: do not let device preference bypass architecture. Decide the operating model first, then the hardware.
Implication
Decision gap
This is enough to reopen the decision. It is no longer credible to treat the request only as personal hardware preference.
I need Mac versus Windows/WSL versus hosted agent workspace compared on total friction, total cost and residual security risk.
ULTIMATE CONCLUSION

I would not approve a Mac rollout for the expanding UX team on this deck alone. I would reopen the request because the visual workflow evidence links endpoint choice to Aurora AI-native product objectives. Authorize bounded IT and InfoSec discovery plus one pilot. If macOS proves materially lower-risk and higher-throughput under enterprise controls, create a UX-specific standard; otherwise invest in a supported Windows/WSL or hosted agent path.
Case reopened. Approval conditional.

slides.name: CTO Review - Supporting Challenges
text: SUPPORTING CHALLENGE LOG / CTO REVIEW
Specific challenges to the Mac approval case
REVIEW STANCE
Friction proven, remedy unproven
The deck makes a credible case for workflow friction. These are the questions I would force before turning that into a supported macOS endpoint standard.
Macs do not create a control plane
Figma pain may be overgeneralized
macOS may remove the WSL boundary, but live canvas automation still depends on app APIs, MCP compatibility, agent permissions, local sockets and tool-specific bridge work.
The bridge evidence is strong, but it could be a Figma integration problem, a local-agent architecture problem, or an endpoint policy problem. The deck collapses those too quickly.
No quantified business loss yet
Security cost is underplayed
I would ask for weekly hours lost, failed loop count, support incidents, manual workaround time, delayed decisions, onboarding drag and any delivery impact.
Mac support means MDM, EDR, DLP, identity, certificates, patching, backup, recovery, procurement, app governance, audit evidence and support readiness.
Local endpoint integration is assumed
Scaling cuts both ways
The deck assumes the future is local app plus local agent. I would compare hosted agent workspaces, cloud dev environments and internal gateway services.
A 4-person UX team makes the workflow issue more material, but also creates a second platform surface unless procurement, security and support are explicit.
Hero-workflow bias
Windows remediation is not exhausted
The current UX lead is unusually technical. The decision should test whether ordinary UX and UR hires can use the model without debugging bridges.
Before approving Macs, compare a productised Windows-native agent service, approved localhost policy, managed tunnel, Figma plugin path or MCP gateway.
AI-native is too broad as a policy test
Precedent risk is real
Almost any advanced tooling request can use that frame. Approval criteria need to define when endpoint exceptions are justified and when platform investment is better.
If UX gets Macs for AI workflow reasons, engineering, data science, research and power users may make adjacent claims. The boundary must be explicit.

The deck proves the current setup creates meaningful friction and should be reopened. It does not yet prove that macOS is the lowest-risk, highest-leverage remedy. The next decision should compare macOS, productised Windows/WSL support and hosted agent infrastructure against measured workflow outcomes and enterprise control cost.
SHARPEST CHALLENGE
Friction proven. Remedy not yet proven.

design.argument: Round 1: The Design Org case should make its decision logic explicit against Platform Org. It should connect the stated position to the available slide evidence and the review criteria: Invest in the workflow path that unlocks design productivity. The visual and textual evidence should be used to distinguish proven facts from interpretation.
claims: Design Org has a coherent position to test.
The side needs evidence tied to decision criteria.
The argument should separate facts, assumptions and required next evidence.
